drifted away from acceptable norms that would garner and keep the trust of citizens now is clouded with uncertainty, all appearances of influence controlled, and quite frankly has gone on tilt, continuously spiraling further and further out of control.  T his type morally incomprehensible behavior is one that 
will almost certainly lead to the decay and destruction of our society and most certainly the removal of the expectation of equality, fairness, and protections under the laws that are supposed to be gu aranteed 
to all United States Citizens.  As a Marine Corps Veteran, Movant harshly condemns this abominable treatment, and would further state that this complete usurping and allowing of the other party to dictate conditions and orders contrary to her being in gross and willful contempt of the standing order prior . 
 
WHEREFORE ALL PREMISES CONSIDERED

RULE 1.09 NOTIFICATION OF RELEVANT PENDING CASES  
�When counsel in a cause is aware of a case pending in another court of this or any other state or 
federal jurisdiction, which lik ely may affect the subject matter or jurisdiction of the cause pending in 
the Chancery Court, such counsel shall immediately notify  the Chancery Court by written notice 
docketed for such other cause .  
The Plaintiff and  her counsel have  not only failed , but have  contumaciously failed to comply with this 
crucial rule of the UNIFORM CHANCERY COURT RULES , and in the act  of concealing their 
already contuma cious  acts have now  seemingly convinced this court to challenge the jurisdiction of 
nearly every item on trial in  Lafayette County Justice Court two separate times,  thus subjecting 
Defendant in both venues to Double Jeopardy. This Amounts to a  clear violation of Defendants 
constitutional rights , both State and Federal ; and a open refusal of his due process rights by asserting 
his guilt and making him prove his innocence , while by every definition exploiting the Defendant and 
in so doing, exploiting the minor child.  
Voluntary dismissal is allowed under M.R.C.P. 41 at any time prior to service by the adverse party of 
an answer or summary judgment, when a civil case is so dismissed and then refiled immediately 
thereafter with no substantial change in the parties or claims, such practice, as an example, may be taken as a willful violation of this rule. Willful violation of this rule may constitute an offense subject 
to suspension and other discipline under Rule 3.4(c) of the Rules of Professional Conduct. Sanctions authorized by thi s rule are cumulative to discipline under the Rules of Professional Conduct. 
 
\n--- Page 221 ---
 
RULE 2.02 PLEADINGS MUST BE FILED BEFORE PRESENTED  
All pleadings, accounts and other papers in any action shall be filed with the Clerk of the proper 
Court before being presented to the Chancellor. If to do so would inflict undue hardship on the attorney, or in emergency matters, the papers may be presented to the Chancellor and marked filed by him as provided in M.R.C.P. 5(e). Thereafter, the said  papers shall be forthwith transmitted by 
the attorney to the proper Clerk 
IN MULTIPLE INSTANCES BY MOTION BEFORE THIS COURT, MOVANT RAISED 
LARGE GRIEVANCES ABOUT  THE FACT THAT ORDERS INCLUDING AGREED TO 
ORDERS WERE GENERATED, AD JUDGED, AND FILED WITH THE OPPOSING 
COUNSEL SIGNATURE AND THE JUDGES SIGNATURE, YET UNSIGNED BY MOVANT THROUGH NO REFUSAL TO SIGN BY MOVANT.  
 
RULE 2.0 3 NO BLANKS IN PLEADINGS  addresses this error, which to this day has remained 
raised via multiple filed documents and motions with this court, yet remains unaddressed and now 
manifest error  
RULE 2.0 3 of the Uniform Chancery Court Rules States the following:  
�No blanks shall be contained in any pleading.�  
 
RULE 2.06 BLANKS IN PLEADINGS MUST BE FILLED IN  
All blanks contained in any pleading must be properly filled in according to the fact or facts before being filed with the clerk or presented for consideration by the Court or Chancellor. If the pleader 
\n--- Page 222 ---
does not know, and is unable to learn, the necessary fact or facts to enable him to fill in such blanks 
accurately, he must so state in his pleading 
 
 
 
RULE 1.11 MOTIONS FOR RECUSAL OF JUDGES  
Any party may move for the recusal of a judge of the chancery court if it appears that the judge�s 
impartially might be questioned by a reasonable person knowing all the circumstances, or for 
other grounds provided in the Code of Judicial Conduct or otherwise as provided by law. A motion seeking recusal shall be filed with an affidavit of the party or the party�s attorney setting forth the factual basis underlying the asserted grounds for recusal and declari ng that the motion is filed in 
good faith and that the affiant truly believes the facts underlying the grounds stated to be true. 
Such motion shall, in the first instance, be filed with the judge who is the subject of the motion 
within 30 days following no tification to the parties of the name of the judge assigned to the case; 
or, if it is based upon facts which could not reasonably have been known to the filing party within such time, it shall be filed within 30 days after the filing party could reasonably  discover the facts 
underlying the grounds asserted. The subject judge shall consider and rule on the motion within 30 days of the filing of the motion, with hearing if necessary. If a hearing is held, it shall be on the record in open court. The denial of  a motion to recuse is subject to review by the Supreme Court 
on motion of the party filing the motion as provided in M.R.A.P. 48B.  
 
UNDER THE MISSISSIPPI RULES OF CRIMINAL PROCEDURE Defendant now 
faces Double Jeopardy based on the same set of facts assert ed and forcing the Defendant to waive 
his Fourth Amendment Right and in doing so forgo his fifth amendment right against self -
\n--- Page 223 ---
incrimination  by ordering Defendant to prove his innocence  and give evidence to the State, or 
forgo visitation with his minor child. Undeserved exploitation of the minor child, by way of making 
a presumably innocent man and father  whom has ongoing matters involving alleged civil rights 
violations , choose between protecting his civil right s seemingly  further under sieg e or surrender 
his rights  in an apparent  barter for due time with  his minor c hild specified out in the current chil d 
visitation agreement with which is f urther supported by his c ontinued compliance with child 
support  obligations continuously being paid to petitioner  pursuant to the current child  support 
agreement and subsequent withholding order . The purpos es served by Rule 2.2 are in accord with 
Mississippi law and the mandates of the Fourth Amendment to the United States Constitution. As 
the United States Supreme Court stated in Giordenello v. United States, 357 U.S. 480, 78 S. Ct. 1245, 2 L. Ed. 2d 1503 ( 1958):  
The Judge s hould not accept without question the affiant�s mere conclusion 
that the person whose arrest is sought has committed a crime�  
 
While being civil in nature and filed in chance ry court, the matter first and foremost is one that 
was brought and proper in the justice court of L afayette County. Thus defendant feels it is proper 
in this case to consider Mississippi  rules of criminal procedure being that it involves a currently 
open and now highly interconnected and intertwined conflict  before the justice court of Lafayette 
county between the plaintiff and defendant  and the prior order given by this court subjects 
movant/defendant to double jeopardy along with deprivation of key due process rights. Thus subject to 1.06(b) decisions regarding this type of matter and its assignment shall be subject to review by the supreme court under M.R.A.P . 21, and appropriate stays shall be entered 
by the trial court to allow such review.   
 
 
RULE 1.06 OF THE UNIFORM CHANCERY COURT RULES DISCUSSES 
\n--- Page 224 ---
ASSIGNMENT OF CASES  WHERE THERE MAY BE A CONFLICT OR 
CHALLENGE OF JURISDICTION 
(A)  In multi -judge districts and courts, all civil cases shall be assigned immediately on the filing of 
the complaint by such method which shall insure that the assignment shall be random, that no 
discernable pattern of assignment exists, and that no person shall know to whom the case will be assigned until it has been assigned. If an attorney or party shall attempt to manipulate or defeat the purpose of this rule, the case shall be reassigned to the judge who would have otherwise received the assignment. If the judge who would have received the case under an assignment in compliance with this rule cannot be determined, a new assignment in compliance with the rule shall be made, excluding the judge t o whom it was incorrectly assigned. Sanctions, 
including costs and attorney�s fees, may be imposed by that judge on reassignment. Such sanctions may also include suspension from practice in the court imposing them for not more than 30 days and referral to the Bar for further discipline. 
(B)  Decisions regarding this rule shall be subject to review by the Supreme Court under M.R.A.P. 21, and appropriate stays shall be entered by the trial court to allow such review. 
(C) In districts where motion days are set in advance with judges specifically assigned, preliminary procedural matters and those matters enumerated under M.R.C.P. 81(d)(1) and (2) may be submitted to the judge assigned such duties, notwithstanding the fact t hat the case has been 
assigned to another judge. Furthermore, by local rule approved by the Supreme Court, the trial court may make special provisions accommodating local needs of  economy and efficiency which 
might otherwise be at variance with this rule.  
 
Movant states that he has continued to raise the same grievances about the plaintiff continuously going on hiatus in withholding the minor child against all pre -dated sig ned orders before this 
court. Movant states that s each motion brought before this court has been prejudicially ignored 
with preferential treatment shown to the petitioner time after time despite the fact that petitioner 
\n--- Page 225 ---
has had a history of filing false re ports  and making the most bizarre, heinous, and defaming claims 
despite the fact that she is the one that has manufactured nearly every �crisis� brought before this 
court which she claimed justified intentionally withholding and depriving the defendant/ respondent  of his minor child, and doing so on her own weeks if not months (as 
demonstrated) before ever noticing this court or filing any petition seeking to suspend visitation.  Defendant/Movant would state t his type of  methodology would amount to a cer tain abhorrent  
miscarriage of the judicial system where long established jurisprudence should always condemn 
and deter.  Yet this court has failed to ever do so much as even address this grievance and instead has been allowing plaintiff to get away with this act each time despite her well documented history of making false reports, reporting false crimes, and stating false claims   
Movant asserts and affirms through multiple recent examples including this cause that there has been a 
clearly shown lack of adherence and care to the equal protections under the laws which are afforded to all citizens under the Fourteenth Amendment to The United States Constitution.  A system designed to provide due process and one that should stay uninfluenceable and non- biased would appear to have 
drifted away from acceptable norms that would garner and keep the trust of citizens now is clouded with uncertainty, all appearances of influence controlled, and quite frankly has gone on tilt, continuously spiraling further and further out of control.  T his type morally incomprehensible behavior is one that 
will almost certainly lead to the decay and destruction of our society and most certainly the removal of the expectation of equality, fairness, and protections under the laws that are supposed to be gu aranteed 
to all United States Citizens.  As a Marine Corps Veteran, Movant harshly condemns this abominable treatment, and would further state that this complete usurping and allowing of the other party to dictate conditions and orders contrary to her being in gross and willful contempt of the standing order prior . 
 
WHEREFORE ALL PREMISES CONSIDERED

to the best of his ability.

allegations in this motion are following; that the defendant

explain to the defendant that he was not permitted 
unauthorized movement about the City of Oxford or Lafayette 
county. No revocation proceedings were initiated at that 
time. Then the additional allegation was that undersigned 
counsel had been notified by both law enforcement and private 
citizens that the defendant presented himself in the City of 
Oxford on Friday October 28, 2022 and Monday October 31st, 
2022 for unauthorized and unscheduled purposes in violation 
of the order of banishment. Specifically, Mr. Reardon 
presented himself at the local Federal Bureau Investigation 
Office on Friday October 28 and then in Shelby's Place, a 
residential neighborhood on the evening of October 31st, 2022 
either which violates the terms of his probation. The State

on the violation of the banishment. So if these allegations

\n=== 2024-12-23 - Order On Motion For Counsel.pdf ===
Pages: 1
\n--- Page 1 ---
Serial: 255315
IN THE SUPREME COURT OF MISSISSIPPI
No. 2024-TS-00839
MATTHEW D. REARDON A/K/A MATTHEW 
REARDONAppellant
 v.  
STATE OF MISSISSIPPI Appellee
ORDER
This matter is before the undersigned Justice on the Motion for Appointment of 
Appellate Counsel filed pro se by Matthew O. Reardon.  No response was filed.  Having 
duly considered this matter, the undersigned Justice finds that, pursuant to Rule 6(c)(1) of 
the Mississippi Rules of Appellate Procedure, this matter should be remanded to the 
Circuit Court of Lafayette County for a period of thirty (30) days for disposition on 
Reardon�s Motion for Appointment of Appellate Counsel.  
IT IS THEREFORE ORDERED that the Motion for Appointment of Appellate 
Counsel is remanded to the Circuit Court of Lafayette County for a period of thirty days 
from the entry of this order, within which time the circuit court shall rule on Reardon�s 
Motion for Appointment of Appellate Counsel.
SO ORDERED.

\n=== 2025-02-01 - CFAPA Press Credentials.pdf ===

\n--- Page 1 ---
PRESS
(Presse / Prensa) 
Reporter/Photographer/Videographer  Credentials 
This certifies that as of __________
Do not hinder, exclude, or block the view of this journalist in the exercise of the long-
established and court-recognized 1st Amendment right to be present at and to photograph 
or film any event in any public place. See : Perry Education Association v. Perry Local 
Educators� Association. (1983)  
1st Amendment:  �Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, 
or prohibiting the free exercise thereof; or abridging the freedom of speech, or of the 
press; or the right of the people peaceably to assemble, and to petition the Government 
for a redress of grievances.� (Enacted by Congress on December 15, 1791.) 
Any officer or public official who deprives another of their 1st Amendment rights is 
personally and departmentally subject to suit for civil damages for deprivation of rights. 
Per 42 U.S.C. � 1983, "every person" who under color of law deprives another person of 
his civil rights is liable for civil damages.) See: Pierson v. Ray, 386 U.S. 547 (1967) � a U.S. Supreme Court case. Also see: Monell v. Department of Social Services , wherein the 
U.S. Supreme Court held that municipalities and local governments can be sued if the action was attributable to an official policy. Law enforcement officers, public officials, 
and public employees do not enjoy absolute immunity.  
Furthermore, Section 1988 of Title 42 of the United States Code provides for 
reimbursement of attorneys' fees to plaintiffs who prevail under Section 1983. See: 
Hughes v. Rowe, 449 U.S. 5, 14 (1980) 
For further legal citations on journalism law, see: www.rcfp.org
 
We The People, in the Internet Age , Are The Free Press!  
Copyright 2014, Constitution First Amendment Press Association. (CFAPA.org)  - All Rights 
Reserv ed. The CFAPA is not liable or responsible for the actions of its independent members.  _________________________________________
is a Reporter/Photographer/Videographer and a member in good standing with the 
Constitution First Amen dment Press Association. (CFAPA.org)

Transcript: Don Matthews GRAMA Request Interaction
Date: Thursday, February 13, 2025
Location: Layton City Hall and Police Department
Subject: GRAMA Request for Records from "Silent Audit" on February 3, 2025
Part 1: City Hall - Initial Visit
Don Matthews: Hello. Is Clinton in?
Receptionist: Excuse me?
Don Matthews: Is Clinton in?
Receptionist: Don't think he's here. He's in a meeting. Can I check real quick for him with the secretary?
Don Matthews: Yes, ma'am.
Receptionist: Just a second. All right. He is not here. We're not sure if he's going to be back today.
Don Matthews: I can give you a list. I got one. Well, then I need somebody else to look this up because 
this was sent to y'all's office. I did a public record request, I guess a GRAMA request.
Receptionist: For?
Don Matthews: You sent it to our office?
Don Matthews: No, I sent it to the police department, to the right person in the police department. They 
sent something over here and it's been hung up over here in y'all's office.
Receptionist: Okay, they called us yesterday morning and they said that the CD was ready for you. Did 
you go over there yesterday at all?
Don Matthews: No, I talked to them yesterday and they told me that it was over at legal on the 911 call 
that it still hadn't been signed off.
Receptionist: Let me make a phone call real quick. I thought they had it ready for you.
Don Matthews: And I was curious about it, and I wanted to speak to somebody here at this office about 
it, because... me being a member of the press, a journalist, I'm still the party involved so, you know, it was 
almost like that was... you know, they didn't want to turn that over because they said they were asking if I 
was a journalist like that would make a difference in whether or not I could get the stuff. It shouldn't.
Receptionist: It shouldn't. Yeah, it's... [makes phone call] Remind me your name.
\n--- Page 2 ---
Don Matthews: Don Matthews.
Receptionist: [on phone] Don Matthews. Hey, look. I'm sorry I called her right now. Where is Aspen at my 
chance? Thanks, [to Don] Hey, it's not that... [back to phone] Did you tell me that that CD wasn't for Don 
Matthews? He is. Okay, sure. Let me tell you. Don't call me back. Okay. Thanks, [to Don] She said she 
thinks it's ready. She's just going to double check.
Don Matthews: Okay.
Receptionist: She'll call me right back.
Don Matthews: Sounds good.
Receptionist: Yeah, and I'm not familiar with the case.
Don Matthews: I understand.
Receptionist: I knew you might be coming, so I know your name, but I don't know what the case is even 
about.
Don Matthews: It was just, it wasn't even really a big, I mean, it was interaction with the Layton police. I 
was standing on a sidewalk filming. I'm a journalist. I believe in the First Amendment. And the bank called 
the police on me for standing on the sidewalk.
Receptionist: The state grant, okay. The Zion's Bank?
Don Matthews: Right here on Fort Lane?
Receptionist: Yes, ma'am.
Don Matthews: I think so. Right there in that shopping center area.
Receptionist: Yeah.
Don Matthews: And I just, you know, I just wanted to see what was said when the officers went inside, 
you know, to the bank workers.
Receptionist: To the bank workers, correct.
Don Matthews: Just to make sure that everything, you know, is handled correctly like I figure it was. I 
make public record requests. I do a lot of public record requests in my line of work.
Receptionist: Yes, ma'am. Yeah. Well, Aspen's right here. She'll call me back. The price of everything is 
something, and I came and I spoke at City Hall on last Thursday about this.
Don Matthews: Uh-huh. $10 per report is...
\n--- Page 3 ---
Receptionist: Yeah, we have to charge that same thing when people pick stuff up from us.
Don Matthews: Right. How long have y'all been doing it? Because that's outrageous.
Receptionist: As long as I've been here. I've been in this department for a little over two years.
Don Matthews: Okay. And I know... some cities charge even more than they charge by the page. We 
used to... it used to be ten dollars for a first amount and then extra if there were photos and stuff and 
then a couple years they revisit.
Don Matthews: So yeah, no, like I've done a lot of different public record requests around the country 
and ten dollars for a report is still like... The problem is, and this is where I brought up at City Council 
meeting the other night, is when it's already being paid for by taxpayer dollars, you know, it's a method 
of transparency, and I believe that charging ten dollars for a report discourages people from wanting to 
look into things, you know, to request things. Because, I mean, really, I mean, it's just $10 per page.
Receptionist: The body cam price is... The $10 per page is for the full... Or for the report. But generally, a 
report's not going to be but, you know, front and back page, you know? And that price is just standard, 
whether it's, you know, no redactions, one redaction. You know, it's just fixed.
Don Matthews: I would like to see that, and I told Clinton at the meeting that I would like to come up 
here or I'd like to speak with him about that, you know, because I think that that needs to be looked at. It 
needs to be adjusted because it's just way out of... I mean, the body cam price is normal. You know, $30 
for a body cam, that's normal. I think the 911 price is very expensive. I mean, a 30-second call, 30 bucks 
for a 911 call, come on.
Receptionist: It's 10 for anything that's paper, so full police report. If there are any photos or anything 
like that, that's included in the 10. But then any and all recordings, it's just that set 30. So if there's one 
911 call or if there's 37 officers with body cam, it's all just that same price. It's just a set.
Don Matthews: So $30 gets me the body cams and the 911 call.
Receptionist: Okay, awesome. It should give you any recordings.
Don Matthews: Okay, so just 30 bucks. Alright, I'll go do it.
Receptionist: Alright. And then they said they've got it ready for you if you just show your ID, you should 
be able to pick it up.
Don Matthews: Show my ID? Okay, well I, why do I need to show ID?
Receptionist: We always ask for ID if we're giving something out of this office. That's just, if you don't 
have it you can talk to my office.
\n--- Page 4 ---
Don Matthews: Yeah, I'll talk to him about it.
Receptionist: If we give something out, we always want to have ID, because we have to say who we gave 
it to, we have to have proof of who we gave it to.
Don Matthews: Right. Yeah, I, um, that's one thing I... the Fourth Amendment, I don't, unless I'm accused 
of committing a crime, ID. I mean, that's, I'm a stickler about that. I don't believe that I should have to 
produce ID just to get a record. That's a strong no for me.
Receptionist: Oh.
Don Matthews: A hard no for me.
Receptionist: Yeah, most people don't have a problem.
Don Matthews: I do. Yeah, it's, but it just gets into more constitutional issues. You know, you shouldn't 
have to produce ID to get a report. I mean, it's...
Receptionist: Right. Do you want me to give Clint your number to have him call you back? Do you want

Don Matthews: Yeah. Um. Is he going to be in today?

Police Supervisor: Okay.
Don Matthews: And I... Never have to. The only time I show ID is if I've committed a crime. I believe in

Don Matthews: [to himself] So apparently they're into a little game playing. Let me walk all the way

Receptionist: Yeah, see, and those guys in police, they didn't know that Mark had court today. We have

Conclusion
This transcript documents Don Matthews' attempt to obtain records related to a police interaction that 
occurred on February 3, 2025, outside Zions Bank. Throughout the interaction, Matthews refuses to show 
ID based on Fourth Amendment principles, while city officials maintain it's standard procedure to request 
ID for all records requests. The matter is ultimately postponed until the following day, with city prosecutor 
Mark Arrington agreeing to review the case first thing in the morning.

February 25, 2025 
RE: COMPLAINT AGAINST THE FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION S FOR 
FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE PUBLIC CORRUPTION AND CIVIL RIGHTS VIOLATIONS 
Dear Inspector General, 
I am submitting this formal complaint to the Office of the Inspector General (OIG) regarding the 
Federal Bureau of Investigation�s (FBI) failure to investigate  documented allegations of public 
corruption, perjury, malicious prosecution, and civil rights violations committed by officials 
in Lafayette County, Mississippi. Despite presenting credible evidence and following proper 
procedures, the FBI neglected its duty to act, thereby enabling wrongful prosecution, false 
imprisonment, and due process violations. 
BACKGROUND 
On October 28, 2022, I attempted to report public corruption and police misconduct involving 
the Holly Springs Police Department and Lafayette County Sheriff�s Department  to the FBI 
office in Oxford, Mississippi. Prior to my visit, I confirmed with the FBI that I could walk in to 
provide evidence of misconduct. However, upon my arrival, I found no agents available, and my 
attempt to report these crimes was later used against me in a wrongful probation 
revocation.  The misconduct included perjury by Lafayett e County Sheriff�s Deputy Kandace 
Beavers, who falsely testified that I had entered the FBI office without permission and without a 
lawful purpose, despite clear audio recordings proving otherwise. This perjury was knowingly 
used by Assistant District Attorney Tiffany Kilpatrick, who petitioned for my probation to be 
revoked based on these false statements. Kilpatrick�s  politically motivated prosecution occurred 
while she was running for Lafayette County Judge, using my case as a campaign advantage. 
The FBI had a duty to investigate violations of federal law, including but not limited to: 
�18 U.S.C. � 24 1 (Conspiracy against Rights)
�18 U.S.C . � 242 (Deprivation of Rights Under Color of Law)
�18 U.S.C. � 1503 (Obstruction of Justice)
Despite these clear violations, the FBI refused to act, effectively shielding  corrupt local 
officials  from accountability.  
SPECIFIC FAILURES OF THE FBI  
1.Failure to Investigate Perjury and Fabricated Evidence
�The FBI was presented with  clear audio recordings contradicting Deputy
Beavers� false testimony , yet it failed to act or intervene.
\n--- Page 2 ---
2 
 2. Neglecting to Investigate Retaliatory Prosecution  
� The FBI ignored  evidence that my prosecution was retaliatory , designed to 
punish me for exercising my  First Amendment rights as a journalist . 
3. Allowing Obstruction of Justice  
� The FBI refused to investigate  Assistant District Attorney Tiffany Kilpatrick�s 
abuse of the judicial process , despite her using  false evidence  to secure my 
wrongful conviction while campaigning for a judgeship.  
4. Pattern of Negligence in Handling Civil Rights Violations  
� The FBI�s refusal to investigate demonstrates a  pattern of systemic failure  to 
address public corruption and law enforcement misconduct in Lafayette County.  
REQUESTED ACTION BY THE OIG  
I respectfully request that the Office of the Inspector General:  
1. Conduct an independent investigation into the FBI�s failure to act on credible 
evidence of public corruption and civil r ights violations.  
2. Initiate disciplinary action against any FBI personnel who neglected their duty to 
investigate these allegations.  
3. Refer the matter to the Civil Rights Division of the U.S. Department of Justice for 
further action.  
4. Ensure that safeguards are put in place to prevent future instances of FBI inaction 
in similar cases.  
Conclusion  
The FBI�s refusal to investigate these  serious violations of federal law  has resulted in a miscarriage 
of justice, wrongful imprisonment, and a violation of my constitutional rights. I urge the Office of 
the Inspector General to take immediate action to correct this failure and hold those responsible 
accountable.  
For the ease and expediting of handling this complaint, I am attaching  supporting evidence  
including  call recordings, audio recordings,  transcripts, and court documents . I will readily provide 
any additional  information , court docu ments , etc. this office  may possibly ne ed. Please confirm 
receipt of this complaint and advise on the next steps regarding the investigation.  
Sincerely,

DOJ Whistleblower Complaint Ignored: Journalist Calls Out
Inspector General for Stonewalling Serious Allegations
by Don Matthews  | Apr 15, 2025  | Breaking News , Breaking News Story , Legal , Politics , Scandals ,
Violations of Civil Rights  | 0 comments
April 12, 2025 � Salt Lake City, UT
Standing outside the U.S. District Court in downtown Salt Lake City, investigative journalist and
constitutional rights advocate Matthew Oliver Reardon called out the U.S. Department of
Justice�s top watchdog today in a blistering press conference. His charge: the DOJ Office of the
Inspector General (OIG) has deliberately ignored a formal whistleblower complaint involving
unlawful surveillance, civil rights violations, and federal retaliation against a journalist.
The complaint, submitted on Wednesday, April 9, 2025, was not Reardon�s first attempt to
alert federal authorities. In fact, it was a brand-new, updated filing that incorporated
additional declarations and evidence stemming from a previous OIG complaint submitted in
early 2025�which also went completely unacknowledged.Whistleblower SHUT  OUT  by DOJ W atchdog � Inspect or Gener al Refus Whistleblower SHUT  OUT  by DOJ W atchdog � Inspect or Gener al Refus ��
13:34
 
Select PageaaCase 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 9     Filed 04/15/25     PageID.39     Page 4 of 8
\n--- Page 5 ---
�This isn�t negligence. This is institutional stonewalling,� Reardon declared. �The OIG was given
hard evidence�sworn statements, supporting documentation, and a full timeline implicating
DOJ-connected actors in surveillance and retaliation. And yet, I�ve received no case number,
no confirmation of receipt, not even a single email response.�
According to Reardon, the complaint includes proof that protected federal data may have
been unlawfully shared with local police in multiple jurisdictions, including in Utah and
Mississippi. The results: doxxing, targeted enforcement actions, and a chilling effect on
protected press activity.
The core allegations involve:
� Improper use of federal surveillance databases (such as NCIC, CJIS, or NLETS)
� Retaliation for lawful journalistic activity, including silent audits and public records
investigations
� Cross-jurisdictional coordination between federal and local officials aimed at discrediting and
silencing a journalist
On April 10, the day after the formal complaint was submitted, Reardon followed up with a
second email�again, no response. Today, Reardon placed a direct call to the OIG�s public
contact line, navigated the phone prompts to reach a live agent, and was sent to voicemail.
Despite leaving a detailed message requesting a same-day response, there was no return call
or follow-up.
�This is the Department of Justice�s internal watchdog,� Reardon said. �If this is how they treat
whistleblower complaints from a journalist with documented constitutional violations, then
how many other legitimate complaints are getting buried? Who else are they protecting?�
The implications go far beyond one individual�s case. Reardon says this silence from the OIG
undermines public trust in government accountability mechanisms and raises serious
concerns about institutional complicity.
�Their job is to investigate misconduct. Instead, they�re helping to conceal it. That makes the
OIG part of the problem, not the solution,� Reardon added.
Reardon�s team has now escalated the matter, preparing certified mail packets to the DOJ
OIG, filing a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to uncover any internal handling of his
complaint, and notifying Judicial Watch, the ACLU, and Congressional oversight bodies.
A formal press release and supporting documentation will be made public in the coming days.
Reardon says the evidence is extensive and disturbing�and he won�t rest until it�s heard.
�If the government refuses to act, the people will,� he concluded. �This is what the First
Amendment was made for. And I intend to use it.�Case 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 9     Filed 04/15/25     PageID.40     Page 5 of 8

RE: **AMENDED COMPLAINT** AGAINST THE FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATIONS FOR 
FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE PUBLIC CORRUPTION AND CIVIL RIGHTS VIOLATIONS AND FOR 
ENGAGING IN RETALIATORY CONDUCT 
Dear Inspector General, 
I am submitting this amended formal complaint to the Office of the Inspector General {OIG) 
regarding the Federal Bureau of Investigation's (FBI) ongoing pattern of misconduct , including: 
(1) failure to investigate documented allegations of public corruption , perjury, malicious 
prosecution, and civil rights violations committed by officials in Lafayette County, Mississippi; 
and (2) active obstruction and retaliation against my attempts to report federal crimes at 
multiple FBI field offices. The amendment includes a new incident that occurred on March 31, 
2025, at the Salt Lake City FBI Field Office, which further demonstrates the Bureau's systematic 
pattern of obstruction , retaliation, and disregard for constitutional rights. 
BACKGROUND FROM ORIGINAL COMPLAINT {FEBRUARY 25. 20251 
On October 28, 2022, I attempted to report public corruption and police misconduct involving 
the Holly Springs Police Department and Lafayette County Sheriffs Department to the FBI office 
in Oxford, Mississippi. Prior to my visit, I confirmed with the FBI that I could walk in to provide 
evidence of misconduct. However, upon my arrival, I found no agents available, and my attempt 
to report these crimes was later used against me in a wrongful probation revocation . The 
misconduct included perjury by Lafayette County Sheriff's Deputy Kandace Beavers, who falsely 
testified that I had entered the FBI office without permission and without a lawful purpose, 
despite clear audio recordings proving otherwise . This perjury was knowingly used by Assistant 
District Attorney Tiffany Kilpatrick , who petitioned for my probation to be revoked based on 
these false statements . Kilpatrick 's politically motivated prosecution occurred while she was 
running for Lafayette County Judge, using my case as a campaign advantage . 
The FBI had a duty to investigate violations of federal law, including but not limited to: Case 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 9     Filed 04/15/25     PageID.42     Page 7 of 8

IN THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT  
FOR THE DISTRICT OF  UTAH  
 
Matthew Reardon  
Plaintiff  
 
v       Cause No: 1:25 -cv-00041 -JCB 
 
City of Layton, Utah , et al,  
Defendants  
 
PLAINTIFF�S MOTION TO LIFT STAY DUE TO CONTINUING HARM  
 
TO THE HONORABLE COURT:  
 
Plaintiff Matthew Oliver Reardon, appearing pro se, respectfully moves this Honorable 
Court to lift the stay entered on March 31, 2025, which presently prohibits the filing of 
any new pleadings or supplemental materials pending review of Plaintiff�s in for ma 
pauperis eligibility. As grounds for this motion, Plaintiff states that he is suffering 
ongoing and compounding harm as a result of retaliatory actions by federal agents and 
state actors, and that continued delay places his safety, liberty, and rights u nder imminent 
threat.  
 
On March 31, 2025 �the same day the Court entered its Order �Plaintiff visited the FBI 
Salt Lake City Field Office to hand -deliver a sworn affidavit pertaining to the criminal 
misconduct of Oxford, Mississippi Police Captain Hildon Ses sums. FBI agents refused to 
accept the affidavit, demanded a background check without lawful basis, threatened 
\n--- Page 2 ---
Plaintiff with arrest, and ordered him off federal property while recording his license 
plate. These actions occurred while Plaintiff was peacefu lly standing in the visitor 
parking lot and exercising his First Amendment rights to petition the government and 
expose misconduct.  
 
The events of March 31 are now documented in a second sworn declaration , which 
Plaintiff intends to file with this court as  Exhibit D, however  Plaintiff has been unable to 
file this due to the stay. The inability to submit material evidence of ongoing retaliation 
not only jeopardizes Plaintiff�s ability to seek justice, but risks creating a procedural 
record that is incomplete  and outdated.  
 
Additionally, Plaintiff is a crime victim as defined under 18 U.S.C. � 3771(e), and 
invokes his rights under the Crime Victims� Rights Act, including the right to be 
reasonably protected from the accused and to proceedings free from unreaso nable delay. 
Plaintiff is actively being targeted and silenced for exposing constitutional violations and 
systemic abuse by both local and federal officials. Judicial inaction at this juncture 
emboldens further retaliation and risks irreparable harm.  
 
Plaintiff therefore respectfully requests that the Court lift or modify its March 31, 2025 
stay order to allow immediate filing of:  
(1) Exhibit D � Plaintiff�s Second Sworn Declaration;  
(2) Plaintiff�s Invocation of Victim Rights Under the Justice for All Act ;  
(3) Plaintiff�s First Amended Complaint;  
\n--- Page 3 ---
Alternatively, Plaintiff asks that the Court authorize chambers submission or under -seal 
lodging of the above documents pending formal acceptance.  
 
WHEREFORE, Plaintiff prays that the stay be lifted or modified to  permit prompt filing 
of critical materials, and for such other relief as the Court deems just and proper.  
 
Respectfully submitted,

MOTION TO RESCIND PRIOR CONSENT TO MAGISTRATE JURISDICTION AND 
TO RECUSE MAGISTRATE JUDGE JARED C. BENNETT  
 
TO THE HONORABLE COURT:  
 
Plaintiff Matthew Oliver Reardon, appearing pro se, respectfully moves this Court to rescind 
his prior consent to magistrate jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. � 636(c)(4), and to recuse 
United States Magistrate Judge Jared C. Bennett pursuant to 28 U.S.C. � 455(a) and (b).  
I. GROUNDS FOR RESCINDING CONSENT  
Plaintiff originally consented to magistrate jurisdiction in good faith and with the belief that 
such assignment would ensure expedited and impartial consideration of his civil rights 
complaint. However, since the Court entered a procedural stay on March 31, 2025, there has 
been no action on Plaintiff�s IFP application, nor on two separately filed emergency motions 
to lift the stay.  
As a result, Plaintiff has been prevented from filing or preserving verified declarations, 
evidentiary supplements, or his First Amended Complaint �despite documenting new and 
ongoing constitutional violations, including retaliation by federal agents.  
            FILED 
     2025 APR 23  
           CLERK 
U.S. DISTRICT COURTCase 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 10     Filed 04/23/25     PageID.44     Page 1 of 3
\n--- Page 2 ---
2 
 Under 28 U.S.C. � 636(c)(4), a district judge may, for good cause shown, vacate a reference 
to a magistrate judge at any time prior to entry of judgment. Plaintiff respectfully asserts 
that good cause now exists, and the reference should be vacated.  
II. GROUNDS FOR RECUSE  
Pursuant to 28 U.S.C. � 455(a), a judge must recuse himself when his impartiality might 
reasonably be questioned. Section 455(b) also mandates recusal when the judge has a 
personal bias or prejudice concerning a party.  
The continued refusal to act �despite two emergency filings outlining clear and ongoing 
constitutional harm �raises a legitimate concern about prejudice, judicial bias, or deliberate 
indifference. Plaintiff need not prove actual bias; the appearance of impartiality alone is 
sufficient.  
The stay has had the effect of silencing Plaintiff, freezing the record, and shielding 
Defendants from scrutiny while real -time retaliation continues. In light of this, Plaintiff 
believes that continued proceedings before Judge Bennett will not be perceived as fair and 
impartial.  
III. REQUEST FOR RELIEF  
Plaintiff respectfully requests that the Court:  
1. Rescind his prior consent to magistrate jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. � 636(c)(4);  
2. Recuse Magistrate Judge Jared C. Bennett from further proceedings under 28 U.S.C. � 455;  
3. Reassign the case to a U.S. District Judge for de novo review of pending motions and 
filings;  Case 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 10     Filed 04/23/25     PageID.45     Page 2 of 3
\n--- Page 3 ---
3 
 4. Rule immediately on Plaintiff�s Motion to Lift Stay and IFP application without further 
delay.  
 
Respectfully submitted,  
 
/s/ Matthew Oliver Reardon  
Plaintiff, Pro Se  
P.O. Box 160144  
Clearfield, UT 84016  
Email: mreardon@wtpnews.org  
Phone: (385) 350 -3998

PLAINTIFF�S MOTION TO LIFT STAY DUE TO CONTINUING HARM  
 
TO THE HONORABLE COURT:  
 
Plaintiff Matthew Oliver Reardon, appearing pro se, respectfully moves this Honorable 
Court to lift the stay entered on March 31, 2025, which presently prohibits the filing of 
any new pleadings or supplemental materials pending review of Plaintiff�s in for ma 
pauperis eligibility. As grounds for this motion, Plaintiff states that he is suffering 
ongoing and compounding harm as a result of retaliatory actions by federal agents and 
state actors, and that continued delay places his safety, liberty, and rights u nder imminent 
threat.  
 
On March 31, 2025 �the same day the Court entered its Order �Plaintiff visited the FBI 
Salt Lake City Field Office to hand -deliver a sworn affidavit pertaining to the criminal 
misconduct of Oxford, Mississippi Police Captain Hildon Ses sums. FBI agents refused to 
accept the affidavit, demanded a background check without lawful basis, threatened Case 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 7     Filed 04/09/25     PageID.29     Page 1 of 3
\n--- Page 2 ---
Plaintiff with arrest, and ordered him off federal property while recording his license 
plate. These actions occurred while Plaintiff was peacefu lly standing in the visitor 
parking lot and exercising his First Amendment rights to petition the government and 
expose misconduct.  
 
The events of March 31 are now documented in a second sworn declaration , which 
Plaintiff intends to file with this court as  Exhibit D, however  Plaintiff has been unable to 
file this due to the stay. The inability to submit material evidence of ongoing retaliation 
not only jeopardizes Plaintiff�s ability to seek justice, but risks creating a procedural 
record that is incomplete  and outdated.  
 
Additionally, Plaintiff is a crime victim as defined under 18 U.S.C. � 3771(e), and 
invokes his rights under the Crime Victims� Rights Act, including the right to be 
reasonably protected from the accused and to proceedings free from unreaso nable delay. 
Plaintiff is actively being targeted and silenced for exposing constitutional violations and 
systemic abuse by both local and federal officials. Judicial inaction at this juncture 
emboldens further retaliation and risks irreparable harm.  
 
Plaintiff therefore respectfully requests that the Court lift or modify its March 31, 2025 
stay order to allow immediate filing of:  
(1) Exhibit D � Plaintiff�s Second Sworn Declaration;  
(2) Plaintiff�s Invocation of Victim Rights Under the Justice for All Act ;  
(3) Plaintiff�s First Amended Complaint;  Case 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 7     Filed 04/09/25     PageID.30     Page 2 of 3
\n--- Page 3 ---
Alternatively, Plaintiff asks that the Court authorize chambers submission or under -seal 
lodging of the above documents pending formal acceptance.  
 
WHEREFORE, Plaintiff prays that the stay be lifted or modified to  permit prompt filing 
of critical materials, and for such other relief as the Court deems just and proper.

Proffered Sworn Declaration and Notice of Victim Status  
 
Re: Reardon v. Layton City et al., Case No. 1:25 -cv-00041 -JCB 
United States District Court for the District of Utah  
 
TO THE HONORABLE COURT:  
 
Comes now the Plaintiff, Matthew Oliver Reardon, pro se, and respectfully submits this 
Proffered Sworn Declaration and Notice of Victim Status for chambers review and post -stay 
docket consideration pursuant to the Justice for All Act of 2004 (18 U.S.C. � 3771), the First 
and Fourth Amendments of the U.S. Constitution, and inherent equitable authority to 
prevent continuing irreparable harm.  
 
This submission is not a motion, and no relief is presently requested. Plaintiff fully 
acknowledges the Court�s March 31, 2025 Order instructing that no further pleadings be 
filed pending judicial review of Plaintiff�s IFP eligibility and initial complaint.  
 
However, Plaintiff must respectfully notify the Court of subsequent events occurring on 
March 31, 2025, that directly relate to the allegations raised in this case and materially 
expand the nature of constitutional violations alleged therein. These events are the subject 
of the enclosed sworn declaration (Exhibit D), which Plaintiff attempted to deliver in person 
to the FBI Salt Lake City Field Office. The FBI refused to  accept the document, threatened 
arrest, and recorded Plaintiff�s license plate in retaliation for peacefully attempting to report 
federal crimes and misconduct connected to this litigation.  
 
Additionally, Plaintiff asserts his legal standing as a crime victim under 18 U.S.C. � 3771(e), 
and invokes the statutory rights therein, including:  
- The right to be treated with fairness and with respect for dignity and privacy;  
- The right to be reasonably protected from the accused;  
- The right to proceedings free from unreasonable delay;  
- And the right to be heard at public proceedings involving the criminal conduct at issue.  
 
Plaintiff alleges that the conduct of Captain Hildon Sessums and certain agents of the FBI �
including retaliation for whistleblower disclosures, suppression of evidence, and 
deprivation of rights under color of law �constitute criminal acts in violation of 18 U.S.C. �� 
241, 242, 1512, and 1513.  
 
In light of these developments, Plaintiff respectfully submits this declaration into the Court�s 
chambers record solely for preservation and post -stay review. Plaintiff does not request any Case 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 8     Filed 04/09/25     PageID.32     Page 1 of 4
\n--- Page 2 ---
ruling or judicial action at this time, but asserts that continued silence or inaction may 
embolden further harm and compound existing retaliation, including through surveillance, 
reputational damage, and barriers to legal redress.  
 
This submission is also being referred to the DOJ Office of Inspector General, the U.S. Senate 
Judiciary Committee, and relevant oversight bodies to ensure transparency and 
accountability.  
 
 
Respectfully submitted,

DECLARATION OF MATTHEW OLIVER REARDON  
Pursuant to 28 U.S.C. � 1746  
I, Matthew Oliver Reardon, declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United 
States of America that the following is true and correct:  
1. I am a journalist and constitutional rights advocate residing in Layton, Utah. I am 
competent to make this declaration  and do so of my own personal knowledge.  
2. On March 31, 2025, I visited the FBI Salt Lake City Field Office in an attempt to deliver 
an affidavit alleging  serious misconduct and potential federal crimes by Captain Hildon 
Sessums of the Oxford, Mississippi Police Department.  
3. Captain Sessums has previously admitted to being a graduate of the FBI National Academy, 
and I believe that  his ties to the FBI may explain the federal government's ongoing 
unwillingness to investigate his conduct or accept reports implicating him in civil rights 
violations and unlawful surveillance.  
4. I have previously experienced refusal, stonewalling, and systemic inaction from the FBI in 
response to my attempts  to report documented abuses involving Mississippi state and local 
officials. I believe this demonstrates a longstanding pattern of disregard or prejudice by the 
Bureau against me.  
5. Upon arrival at the Salt Lake FBI office on March 31, 2025, I was not allowed past the 
outer visitor gate.FBI agents refused to even approach to retrieve my affidavit or engage in 
discussion. They instead demanded my driver's license and personal identific ation for the 
purpose  of running a full background check, before they would even accept a piece of paper 
from me.  
6. I was there f or lawful purposes,  attempting to submit documented evidence and a sworn  
statement alleging criminal activity by a law enforcement official.  
7. I explicitly refused to submit to a background check, citing my Fourth Amendment right 
against  unreasonable  search and seizure. I told them that no victim of a crime should be 
required to give up their constitutional rights to report criminal conduct.  Case 1:25-cv-00041-JCB     Document 8     Filed 04/09/25     PageID.34     Page 3 of 4
\n--- Page 4 ---
8. The FBI agent  became hostile and refused to engage unless I complied with this arbitrary 
and unconstitutional  demand. This procedure, which he claimed was "policy," served to 
chill my First Amendment rights, as I was ultimately prevented from subm itting  a crimin al 
complaint or  my affidavit  due to their refusal to accept it without unlawfully searching me 
first. 
9. I began reporting on the event live, standing in the publicly accessible visitor parking lot 
outside the gate of the  FBI field office. I was peacefully reading from my declaration on 
camera when two FBI agents approached me without warning or conversation and ordered 
me to leave immediately, under threat of arrest for trespassing.  
10. When I asserted my right to be present in the public visitor area as a journalist and civilian .  
I pointed out  that taxpayer -funded federal property is not private, however they insisted the 
parking lot was "FBI -owned private property" and that I was no longer welcome. Their 
only response was: "If you don't leave, you will be charged."  
11. In addition, an agent walked around my car to the rear and recorded my license plate 
number  further escalating  the surveillance, intimidation, and harassment against me.  
12. I believe that this act was retaliatory, and that the FBI's actions -past and present -reflect a 
deliberate effort to  suppress my speech, evade accountability, and shield Sessums from 
investigation due to institutional ties and complicity.  
13. I believe this encounter, as well as prior conduct, establishes:  
- A violation of my First Amendment right to petition the government and report as a journalist.  
- A violation of my Fourth Amendment right against suspicion -less search.  
- A retaliatory pattern against a known journalist and rights advocate.  
- Probable unlawful sharing of surveillance/intelligence with Sessums by the FBI or its agents.  
14. I reserve all rights to pursue appropriate legal action, including constitutional claims and 
discovery of FBI communications with Oxford, Mississippi law enforcement.  
 
Executed on this  1st day of April, 2025  at 0700 . 
 
_________________________________  
             Matthew Oliver Reardon

  IN THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR 
THE DISTRICT OF UTAH  
  
MATTHEW REARDON ,  
PLAINTIFF,    
 
v.        Civil Action No. 1:25-cv-0004 1-JCB 
  
CITY OF LAYTON, UTAH ;  
LAYTON POLICE DEPARTMENT ; 
CITY OF OXFORD, MISSISSIPPI ;  
OXFORD POLICE DEPARTMENT.    
CAPTAIN HILDON SESSUMS,  
(Individual  and official capacities );

(Individual and official capacities );

(Individual and official capacity );  
MARK ARRINGTON,  
(Individual and official capacity );   
JOHN DOES 1 -139,  
DEFENDANTS.   
  
 
 
   
FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF     
JURY TRIAL DEMANDED  
  
  
  
 
  
\n--- Page 2 ---
2  
  I. INTRODUCTION  
1. This is a civil rights action brought under 42 U.S.C. �� 1983 and 1985 for declaratory, 
injunctive, and monetary relief arising from coordinated surveillance, retaliatory conduct, 
defamation, and obstruction of records access by government officials in Lay ton, Utah 
and Oxford, Mississippi.  
  
2. Plaintiff Matthew Reardon is a journalist, author, and public transparency advocate. He 
brings this action to redress harm caused by state and local officials acting in concert to 
silence and retaliate against him for protected First Amendment activity.  
  
II. JURISDICTION AND VENUE  
3. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. � 1331 and civil rights 
jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. � 1343.  
  
4. The Court has supplemental jurisdiction over related state law claims pursuant to 28 
U.S.C. � 1367.  
  
5. Venue is proper in this District under 28 U.S.C. � 1391(b) because a substantial part of 
the events giving rise to the claims occurred in Utah.  
III. PARTIES  
6. Plaintiff Matthew Reardon resides in Davis County, Utah, and is an independent 
investigative journalist, constitutional rights advocate, and author with his first book set 
to  
\n--- Page 3 ---
3  
  be published in April 2025. Plaintiff actively engages in public transparency work and 
journalism targeting government accountability.  
  
7. Defendant City of Layton is a municipal entity located in Davis County, Utah. It 
establishes policies, procedures, and practices for City departments, including the Police 
Department, and is responsible for ensuring that its employees comply with federal a nd 
state law.  
  
8. Defendant Layton Police Department is a division of the City of Layton that operates 
under policies, practices, and customs established by the City of Layton.  
  
9. Defendant City of Oxford, Mississippi is a municipal corporation responsible for 
oversight of its police department and municipal employees, and for establishing policies, 
procedures, and practices ensuring compliance with federal and state law.  
  
10. Defendant Oxford Police Department is a division of the City of Oxford that operates 
under policies, practices, and customs established by the City of Oxford.  
  
11. Defendant Captain Hildon Sessums is a sworn officer with Oxford Police Department 
who, at all times relevant to this Complaint, acted under color of state law. He personally 
participated in obtaining and disseminating Plaintiff's private information and de famatory 
statements about Plaintiff.  
  
\n--- Page 4 ---
4  
  12. Defendant Chief Jeff McCutchen is the Chief of Oxford Police Department who, at all 
times relevant to this Complaint, acted under color of state law. He personally 
participated in and/or had knowledge of, approved, and ratified the conduct of Defendant 
Sessums.  
  
13. Defendant Lieutenant Riley Richins is a Layton Police Department officer who, at all 
times relevant to this Complaint, acted under color of state law. He personally 
participated in disseminating false and defamatory information about Plaintiff throughout  
Layton City government.  
  
14. Defendant Mark Arrington is Layton City Prosecutor who, at all times relevant to this 
Complaint, acted under color of state law. He personally participated in obstructing  
Plaintiff's access to public records under Utah's Government Records Access and  
Management Act (GRAMA) and imposing unlawful conditions on that access.  
  
15. Defendants JOHN DOES 1 -139 are individuals whose names and capacities are presently 
unknown but who were direct recipients of a department -wide internal email sent by 
Defendant Lieutenant Riley Richins on February 5, 2025, containing false and 
defamatory i nformation about Plaintiff. These individuals may have further disseminated 
or acted upon that information. Plaintiff will amend this Complaint to substitute their true 
names and capacities when ascertained.  
 
\n--- Page 5 ---
5  
  IV . FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS  
A. Background  
16. Plaintiff Matthew Reardon relocated to Layton, Utah on February 1, 2025, continuing his 
work under the pseudonym "Don Matthews" to protect his safety.  
  
17. Plaintiff used the name "Don Matthews" when submitting GRAMA requests or engaging 
with public officials due to prior harassment and intimidation related to his journalism 
and transparency advocacy.  
18. No part of Plaintiff's online presence �including YouTube, Facebook, or public records � 
contained identifying information tying "Don Matthews" to "Matthew Reardon." Plaintiff 
took specific measures to maintain this separation for his personal safety.  
  
B. Unlawful Surveillance and Privacy Invasion  
19. On or before February 4, 2025, Oxford Police Captain Hildon Sessums obtained 
Plaintiff's private Utah address and real name through means that could not have been 
lawful public channels.  
  
20. Plaintiff moved into his Layton residence late on February 1, 2025, and had not yet 
disclosed this address to any public or private entity, had not updated any government 
records with this address, and had not even memorized it himself, relying on a text 
message from his landlord when he needed the address.  
  
21. On February 5, 2025, Defendant Sessums sent an unsolicited email to multiple recipients 
at Layton Police Department containing Plaintiff's full legal name, exact Utah residential 
\n--- Page 6 ---
6  
  address, criminal history, and commentary suggesting Plaintiff was dangerous and in need 
of monitoring.  
  
22. In this email, Sessums falsely stated that Plaintiff's address was obtained through  
"research," despite the fact that the address was not available in any public database, 
record , or resource at that time. This demonstrates that Sessums necessarily obtained 
Plaintiff's private residential address through either unlawful surveillance, illegal access 
to confidential records, or unauthorized misuse of governmental resources.  
  
23. Sessums' email specifically identified Plaintiff's new address as 1513 W 1960 N, Layton, 
UT 84041, the exact address Plaintiff had moved to just days before.  
  
24. Defendant Chief McCutchen, as Sessums' superior, knew or should have known of 
Sessums' actions and approved of, ratified, and failed to remedy this unlawful conduct 
when directly confronted about it by Plaintiff in a subsequent phone conversation on  
March 27, 2025.  
 
C. Defamation and False Statements  
25. On February 5, 2025, Lieutenant Riley Richins of Layton PD forwarded Sessums' email 
internally to 139 Layton City employees with an added comment that falsely stated that  
Plaintiff had been "arrested in 2017 for stalking the Mayor in Oxford."  
  
\n--- Page 7 ---
7  
  26. This statement was categorically false. Plaintiff was arrested in 2017 for an incident 
involving an alleged private individual �not anything to do with the Mayor of Oxford or 
any other public official.  
  
27. Richins' statement constituted defamation per se, as it falsely alleged criminal conduct 
involving a public official. The statement was made with either knowledge of its falsity or 
reckless disregard for its truth, as Richins failed to verify this informat ion before 
disseminating it to 139 city employees.  
  
28. The widespread dissemination of this false statement throughout Layton City government 
created a hostile environment for Plaintiff and impeded his ability to engage with 
government officials and exercise his First Amendment rights.  
  
D. GRAMA Obstruction and Retaliation  
29. On February 20, 2025, Plaintiff visited Layton City Hall to follow up on an overdue 
GRAMA request for police reports, 911 call logs, and officer body cam footage from a 
prior incident where he was recording video from a public sidewalk.  
  
30. Layton PD refused to release any records unless Plaintiff showed government -issued ID, 
a condition not required under Utah GRAMA law, Utah Code � 63G -2-101, et seq.  
  
31. Plaintiff met with City Prosecutor Mark Arrington to attempt to resolve the issue. During 
this meeting, Arrington unlawfully implied that use of the alias "Don Matthews" could be 
\n--- Page 8 ---
8  
  criminal if used on official documents �despite no fraud, impersonation, or 
misrepresentation having occurred.  
  
32. Arrington further stated he had to personally approve whether records would be released, 
contradicting prior communications from Layton's records division, and thereby imposing 
additional, non -statutory barriers to Plaintiff's records access.  
  
33. On February 21, 2025, Plaintiff received a phone call from Defendant Mark Arrington.  
During the call, Arrington referred to Plaintiff as "Matthew," demonstrating knowledge of  
Plaintiff's legal identity despite Plaintiff having used only "Don" in prior 
communications.  
  
34. When Plaintiff challenged this, Arrington responded that he knew Plaintiff's legal name 
was Matthew Reardon from Lafayette County, Mississippi and referenced Plaintiff's 
unrelated legal issues in Galveston, Texas �clearly indicating access to sensitive, 
nonpublic background information.  
  
35. Arrington told Plaintiff that his requested records were ready but would only be released 
if Plaintiff came to the Layton Police Department in person and presented government 
issued  identification. When Plaintiff requested electronic transmission or third -party 
pickup �options available under GRAMA �Arrington refused both alternatives, insisting 
on Plaintiff's personal appearance with ID.  
  
\n--- Page 9 ---
9  
  36. This demand constituted deliberate intimidation and retaliation, especially in light of 
Plaintiff's previous experience being lured to a police station in Galveston, Texas under 
false pretenses to retrieve his car keys, only to be arrested based on a warra nt from  
Lafayette County, Mississippi �an incident currently subject to separate litigation.  
  
37. As a direct result of this coercive behavior, Plaintiff was forced to abandon his request for 
non-privileged, lawfully accessible public records.  
  
38. On March 13, 2025, Plaintiff submitted a detailed GRAMA request asking for call logs, 
emails, and communications between February 3 and March 13, 2025, involving his  
name or pseudonym.  
  
39. Layton City failed to acknowledge the request until the legal deadline approached. On 
March 25, 2025, the tenth business day, Layton City Recorder Kimberly Reed produced 
only a call log showing calls made to or from Plaintiff's own phone number �information 
he already possessed �rather than the internal phone records and communications he had 
requested.  
  
40. Reed did produce emails including Sessums' February 5 email and Richins' subsequent 
dissemination, which is how Plaintiff first learned of the defamatory statements made 
about him.  
  
\n--- Page 10 ---
10  
  41. Layton City also withheld a known call recording involving Mark Arrington, which a 
records employee had previously acknowledged existed.  
  
42. On March 28, 2025, Plaintiff called Layton City to follow up with Reed about the missing 
items from his GRAMA request.  
  
43. Plaintiff was informed by Legal staff that because of "pending litigation" (which had not 
yet been filed), he was only allowed to speak with Mark Arrington �a named future 
defendant in this lawsuit �for anything involving GRAMA, creating an unethical conflict 
of interest and effectively blocking access to a neutral records official.  
  
E. Coordinated Efforts Between Jurisdictions  
44. On March 27, 2025, Plaintiff confronted Defendant Chief Jeff McCutchen directly in a 
recorded phone call, explicitly detailing the impossibility of Defendant Sessums's lawful 
acquisition of Plaintiff's private residential address.  
  
45. Despite Plaintiff's clear and specific allegation that Sessums had engaged in unlawful 
surveillance or unauthorized acquisition of Plaintiff's personal information, Chief 
McCutchen failed to deny the allegation, failed to provide any plausible explanation,  and 
explicitly refused Plaintiff's request to initiate an internal investigation into Sessums's 
misconduct.  
46. McCutchen's refusal to act demonstrates a deliberate indifference and tacit approval of 
Defendant Sessums's constitutional violations, further evidencing the existence of a 
\n--- Page 11 ---
11  
  widespread pattern, practice, and policy within the Oxford Police Department and its 
municipal governance to tolerate, condone, and facilitate constitutional abuses against 
individuals exercising protected First Amendment activities.  
  
47. The coordinated actions between Oxford, Mississippi officials and Layton, Utah 
officials �specifically the transfer of unlawfully obtained personal information, the 
concerted effort to characterize Plaintiff as dangerous, and the mutual obstruction of 
Plaintiff's transparency efforts �demonstrate a conspiracy to deprive Plaintiff of his 
constitutional rights.  
F. Additional Incidents Demonstrating Pattern of Retal iation and Obstr uction   
48. On March 31, 2025, Plaintiff visited the FBI Salt Lake City Field Office to submit an 
affidavit alleging serious misconduct and potential federal crimes by Captain Hildon 
Sessums of the Oxford Police Department.  
 
49. Plaintiff sought to report evidence of unauthorized surveillance, civil rights violations, 
and the unlawful acquisition and dissemination of his private residential information as 
detailed in previous paragraphs of this Complaint.  
 
50. The timing of this attempt was significant, as it occurred after Plaintiff's unsuccessful 
attempts to have Defendant McCutchen investigate Sessums' misconduct and after 
Plaintiff's discovery of the apparent coordination between Oxford and Layton officials.   
 
\n--- Page 12 ---
12  
  51. Despite Plaintiff's lawful purpose, FBI personnel refused to accept Plaintiff's affidavit or 
supporting documentation unless Plaintiff first submitted to a full background check and 
provided his driver's license for identification verification.  
 
52. Plaintiff asserted his Fourth Amendment right against unreasonable search and seizure, 
explaining that requiring crime victims to surrender constitutional rights as a 
precondition to reporting criminal conduct creates an unlawful barrier to justice.  
 
53. After FBI personnel refused to accept the documentation, Plaintiff began recording in 
the publicly accessible visitor parking lot, narrating the events that had transpired and 
reading from his declaration while standing outside the secure perimeter.  
 
54. Two FBI agents then approached Plaintiff without warning and ordered him to leave 
immediately under threat of arrest for trespassing, despite Plaintiff's presence in a 
publicly accessible area designated for visitors.  
 
55. The agents claimed the public visitor parking lot was "FBI -owned private property" and 
that Plaintiff was "no longer welcome," stating "If you don't leave, you will be charged."  
 
56. Additionally, an agent walked around Plaintiff's vehicle and recorded his license plate 
number, escalating the surveillance and intimidation.  
 
\n--- Page 13 ---
13  
  57. Plaintiff believes this hostile response was connected to Defendant Sessums' relationship 
with the FBI, as Sessums has previously identified himself as a graduate of the FBI 
National Academy, a potential explanation for how Sessums obtained Plaintiff's pri vate 
information and the apparent coordination in suppressing Plaintiff's speech and 
transparency efforts.  
 
58. This incident represents a continuation of the pattern of retaliation, obstruction, and 
suppression of Plaintiff's constitutional rights that began with the actions of the named 
Defendants and has expanded to effectively block Plaintiff's access to law enf orcement 
complaint mechanisms.  
 
59. The FBI's conduct reinforces the allegations that Defendants have engaged in a far -
reaching conspiracy to silence Plaintiff's First Amendment activities and prevent 
accountability for the constitutional violations described herein.  
 
V . CLAIMS FOR RELIEF  
COUNT I :  First Amendment Retaliation (42 U.S.C. � 1983)  
Against Sessums, Richins, Arrington, McCutchen, and John Does 1 -139 
 
 
60. Plaintiff re -alleges and incorporates by reference all preceding paragraphs as if fully set 
forth herein.  
  
61. The First Amendment to the United States Constitution protects the rights to free speech, 
free press, and to petition the government for redress of grievances.  
\n--- Page 14 ---
14  
    
62. Plaintiff engaged in constitutionally protected activities by:  
a) Conducti ng occasional  "first amendment audits" of public institutions, which 
courts have recognized as protected expressive conduct;  
b) Filing GRAMA requests seeking public information about government 
operations;  
c) Working as a journalist and transparency advocate documenting government 
activities;  
d) Planning publication of a book exposing government misconduct.  
  
63. Defendants took adverse actions against Plaintiff, including:  
a) Unlawfully surveilling him and obtaining his private residential address;  
b) Disseminating false and defamatory information about him to government 
officials;  
c) Imposing unlawful barriers to his access to public records;  
d) Creating a hostile environment among Layton City employees toward him; 
and  
e) Attempting to intimidate him through requiring his physical presence at the 
police department.  
  
64. There is a direct causal relationship between Plaintiff's protected activities and 
Defendants' adverse actions, as evidenced by:  
a) The timing of Defendants' actions occurring immediately after Plaintiff 
began conducting transparency activities in Layton;  
\n--- Page 15 ---
15  
  b) Defendants' explicit references to Plaintiff's First Amendment activities in 
their communications;  
c) The extraordinary efforts taken to identify, monitor, and label Plaintiff; 
and 
d) Defendants' deviation from standard procedures in handling Plaintiff's 
records requests.  
  
65. Defendants' actions would deter a person of ordinary firmness from continuing to engage 
in the protected activity, as demonstrated by Plaintiff's forced abandonment of his records 
requests.  
  
66. As a direct and proximate result of Defendants' unlawful conduct, Plaintiff has suffered 
actual damages including emotional distress, reputational harm, and deprivation of his 
constitutional rights.  
  
COUNT II:  Fourth Amendment - Unlawful Seizure and Surveillance (42  U.S.C. � 1983)  
Against Sessums and McCutchen (individual capacities)

forth herein.  
  
68. The Fourth Amendment protects the right of people to be secure in their persons, houses, 
papers, and effects against unreasonable searches and seizures.

publicly available or disclosed to any public or governmental entity at the time  
\n--- Page 16 ---
16  
  Sessums obtained it.

authorization, warrant, or legitimate law enforcement purpose, as conclusively evidenced

officials without any legitimate law enforcement purpose.

Fourth Amendment violation by refusing to investigate it when directly informed by

actual damages including emotional distress, fear for his personal safety, and deprivation

\n--- Page 17 ---

Against Sessums, Richins, Arrington, McCutchen, and John Does 1 -139

departments; and

constitutional violations when informed of them.

Against Sessums, Richins, and John Does 1 -139 (individual capacities)

issue" requiring monitoring; and 
b) Richins falsely stated that Plaintiff had been "arrested in 2017 for stalking the 
Mayor in Oxford."

conduct, specifically stalking a public official.  
  
\n--- Page 20 ---
20  
  88. The statements were published to third parties when:  
a) Sessums sent the email to multiple Layton officials; and 
b) Richins forwarded the email with his additional false content to 139 city 
employees.

a) The statements were knowingly or recklessly false;  
b) The dissemination went beyond any legitimate law enforcement purpose; and 
c) The widespread distribution to 139 recipients exceeded any qualified privilege.

acted with reckless disregard for their truth, as evidenced by:  
a) The complete lack of factual basis for the claim that plainti ff was arrested and  
charged with  "stalking the Mayor"  in 2017 ;  
b) The failure to verify this serious allegation before broadcasting it; and 
c) The unnecessary breadth of the dissemination.  
  
91. As a direct and proximate result of Defendants' defamatory statements, Plaintiff has 
suffered actual damages to his reputation, emotional distress, and impairment of his 
ability to effectively engage with government officials.

COUNT IX:  GRAMA Violations (Utah Code � 63G -2-101, et seq.)  
Against Layton City and Arrington  
109. Plaintiff re -alleges and incorporates by reference all preceding paragraphs as if fully set 
forth herein.  
110. Utah's Government Records Access and Management Act (GRAMA) establishes the right 
of access to public records with limited exceptions.  
\n--- Page 25 ---
25  
  111. GRAMA does not require requesters to present identification to access  nonprivate 
records.  
  
112. GRAMA does not require requesters to appear in person to receive records.  
  
113. GRAMA establishes specific timelines for responding to records requests.  
  
114. Defendants violated GRAMA by:  
a) Requiring Plaintiff to present government issued  identification to access non -
private records;  
b) Refusing to provide records electronically or through a third -party designee;  
c) Failing to properly respond to Plaintiff's March 13, 2025 request within 
statutory timelines ; 
d) Withholding responsive records without statutory justification; and 
e) Creating additional non -statutory barriers to records access.  
  
115. As a direct and proximate result of Defendants' GRAMA violations, Plaintiff has 
been deprived of his statutory right to access public records and has been forced 
to abandon legitimate records requests.

COUNT XIII:  Violations of the Utah Constitution  
Against All Defendants  
121. Plaintiff re -alleges and incorporates by reference all preceding paragraphs as if fully set 
forth herein.  
 
\n--- Page 30 ---

  122. Article I, Section 1 of the Utah Constitution guarantees that "All men have the inherent 
and inalienable right to enjoy and defend their lives and liberties; to acquire, possess and 
protect property..."  
  
123. Article I, Section 7 of the Utah Constitution provides that "No person shall be deprived of 
life, liberty or property, without due process of law."  
  
124. Article I, Section 14 of the Utah Constitution protects against "unreasonable searches and 
seizures" and violation of the "right of the people to be secure in their persons, houses, 
papers and effects."  
  
125. Article I, Section 15 of the Utah Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and of the 
press.  
  
126. Defendants violated Plaintiff's rights under Article I, Section 1 by:  
a) Interfering with his liberty to move freely without surveillance;  
b) Obstructing his right to acquire information through public records; and  
c) Intruding upon his privacy and property interests in his residential 
information.

a) Imposing arbitrary barriers to his access to public records;  
\n--- Page 31 ---
31  
  b) Creating a system where his only point of contact was a defendant with a 
conflict of interest; and  
c) Providing no meaningful process to challenge these restrictions.  
  
128. Defendants violated Plaintiff's rights under Article I, Section 14 by:  
a) Unlawfully obtaining his private residential address through unspecified 
surveillance or database access;  
b) Seizing his private information without legal authority or legitimate purpose;  
c) Disseminating this information without justification.  
  
129. Defendants violated Plaintiff's rights under Article I, Section 15 by:  
a) Retaliating against him for protected speech and press activities;  
b) Creating a chilling effect on his future journalistic endeavors; and  
c) Impeding his ability to gather information for public transparency purposes.  
  
130. These violations provide a basis for relief directly under the Utah Constitution 
because:  
a) The violations were flagrant;  
b) Existing remedies may not fully address the Utah constitutional injuries; an d  
c) Equitable relief is needed to prevent future violations of state constitutional 
rights.  
  
\n--- Page 32 ---
32  
  131. As a direct and proximate result of Defendants' violations of the Utah

Against All Defendants  
 
145. Plaintiff re -alleges and incorporates by reference all preceding paragraphs as if fully set

a) The lawfulness of obtaining and disseminating Plaintiff's private residential 
address;  
\n--- Page 36 ---
36  
  b) The constitutionality of Defendants' coordinated surveillance of Plaintiff;  
c) The lawfulness of Layton City's imposition of identification requirements for
